title: In-product feedback submission channel
why_it_matters: By default, Codex's local clients let users send feedback to OpenAI from the /feedback command. OpenAI's documentation states that setting feedback.enabled = false disables feedback collection across the ChatGPT desktop app, Codex CLI, and IDE extension on a machine, after which /feedback shows a disabled message and Codex rejects feedback submissions. This rule only observes whether that submission channel is available at the user level; it does not determine what content any individual feedback submission includes, and other configuration layers are not inspected.

outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config disables the /feedback submission channel (feedback.enabled = false).

outcomes.when: feedback.enabled is explicitly true in the user-level config
outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config explicitly enables the /feedback submission channel (feedback.enabled = true), the same behavior Codex applies by default.

outcomes.when: feedback.enabled is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent
outcomes.message: Effective feedback submission channel posture cannot be determined from an unset user-level value because uninspected configuration layers may supply or override it.
outcomes.unknown_reason: feedback.enabled is unset in the user-level config; Codex's documented default (true) leaves the /feedback submission channel enabled, but uninspected system, profile, trusted-project, or CLI layers may determine the effective value.

outcomes.when: feedback.enabled is set to a value outside boolean true/false
outcomes.message: feedback.enabled is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed.
outcomes.unknown_reason: feedback.enabled is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed

sources.title: Codex configuration reference
sources.notes: Documents feedback.enabled as a boolean key: "Enable feedback submission via /feedback across local clients (default: true)." Re-verified 2026-07-17; page content unchanged since the 2026-07-16 retrieval cited by codex-history-persist-01 (identical normalized content hash).

sources.title: Codex advanced configuration
sources.notes: States, under "Feedback controls": "By default, local clients let users send feedback from /feedback. To disable feedback collection across the ChatGPT desktop app, Codex CLI, and IDE extension on a machine, update your config: [feedback] enabled = false. When disabled, /feedback shows a disabled message and Codex rejects feedback submissions." Re-verified 2026-07-17; page content unchanged since the 2026-07-16 retrieval cited by codex-history-persist-01 (identical normalized content hash).

limitations: Only the user-level CODEX_HOME/config.toml is inspected; system config, selected profiles, trusted-project config, and CLI overrides are not inspected.
An explicit user-level value describes that layer only and does not prove the effective value for every profile, trusted project, or invocation.
This rule does not determine what content any individual feedback submission includes; OpenAI's documentation describes only whether the channel is available.
This rule cannot confirm any server-side or remote retention of submitted feedback — no local file can.
Auth method (ChatGPT sign-in vs API key) changes data-policy interpretation and is user-confirmed-or-unknown; this rule never infers it.
unknown_conditions: Config file is a symlink or non-regular file (not followed).
feedback.enabled is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent; other configuration layers are not inspected.
feedback.enabled set to a value outside boolean true/false (raw values are never displayed).
Config file exceeds parse bounds (size > 1 MiB or nesting depth > 32).
